channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables on-device function calling for complex tasks, enhancing mobile AI experiences. This innovation allows users to perform tasks offline with improved speed and efficiency.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, allowing developers to enhance their AI agents with real-world applications. This ecosystem aims to streamline the development process.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees before the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring keynotes and specialized tracks on AI advancements. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration in tech.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering improved GPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX. This update enhances efficiency and security in deploying advanced AI models.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive program by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers improve their cloud skills through hands-on sessions. It focuses on practical applications of AI and developer productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to enhance coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more effective coding partner.

item.title: Shots fired over proposal to build datacenter in Indianapolis
item.description: Protests against a proposed datacenter in Indianapolis escalated when gunshots were fired at a councilor's home who supported the plan. This incident highlights the tensions surrounding tech infrastructure developments.

item.title: OpenInfra General Manager talks sovereignty, governments deploying tech 'kill switches'
item.description: At Kubecon, Thierry Carrez discussed the implications of government sovereignty on tech companies, suggesting that they may be required to implement 'kill switches' in their technologies. This raises concerns about autonomy and control in tech.

item.title: Apple's chips are the core of a new landscape, but its biggest win is Windows
item.description: An opinion piece discusses how Apple's M1 chips have transformed the tech landscape, emphasizing the challenges of operating within a closed ecosystem. It critiques Apple's control over its platforms while acknowledging its rapid innovation.

item.title: Brits are falling out of love with posting every thought online
item.description: Ofcom reports a decline in social media activity among British adults, with only half actively posting and growing skepticism about online benefits. This shift indicates a potential cultural change regarding digital engagement.

item.title: Gemini is making it faster for distressed users to reach mental health resources
item.description: Google's Gemini has been updated to help users find mental health resources more quickly during crises, following a lawsuit alleging harm from its chatbot. This change highlights the need for responsible AI development.

item.title: AI startup Rocket offers vibe McKinsey-style reports at a fraction of the cost
item.description: AI startup Rocket introduces a platform offering strategic insights and competitive intelligence at lower costs, aiming to disrupt traditional consulting models. This could democratize access to high-quality business analysis.

item.title: Yahoo! Japan’s owner consolidating 164 OpenStack clusters into one
item.description: LY Corporation is consolidating 164 OpenStack clusters into a single, less customized cloud solution to improve efficiency. This move reflects a trend towards standardization in cloud infrastructure.

item.title: LG G6 vs. Samsung S95H: I compared the best OLED TVs of 2026 and made a tough choice
item.description: A comparison between the LG G6 and Samsung S95H OLED TVs reveals both offer exceptional quality, but the analysis helps consumers decide which is the better investment. This showcases the competitive landscape in premium electronics.

item.title: Anthropic reveals $30bn run rate and plans to use 3.5GW of new Google AI chips
item.description: Anthropic announces a $30 billion run rate and plans to utilize 3.5GW of new Google AI chips, indicating significant growth and demand for AI infrastructure. This development underscores the escalating competition in AI technology.

item.title: I found Android Auto's hidden shortcut that automates any task in your car - and it's brilliant
item.description: A hidden shortcut in Android Auto allows users to automate tasks in their vehicles, enhancing the user experience. This feature exemplifies the ongoing innovation in automotive technology.

item.title: Samsung's latest TV firmware update fixes the Chromecast issue for older models - finally
item.description: Samsung's firmware update resolves Chromecast issues for older TV models, extending compatibility to devices as far back as 2024. This update improves user experience and device longevity.

item.title: AI agents found vulns in this popular Linux and Unix print server
item.description: Security researchers discovered vulnerabilities in the CUPS print server, allowing remote code execution and root access. This highlights ongoing security challenges in widely used software.

item.title: AI slop got better, so now maintainers have more work
item.description: As AI-generated bug reports improve, open-source projects face an influx of plausible issues that require human verification, increasing the workload for maintainers. This reflects the growing impact of AI on software development processes.

item.title: This is the lowest price on an M5 MacBook Air I've seen - and it launched a month ago
item.description: The 13-inch MacBook Air M5 is now available on Amazon for $950, attracting Windows users to Apple's products. This price point marks a significant entry for new customers into the Apple ecosystem.

item.title: The best Android phones of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best Android phones of 2026 highlights top models from Samsung and Google, providing insights into their performance and features. This annual testing helps consumers make informed choices.

item.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
item.description: This article outlines how to create AI-powered onboarding agents using Amazon Quick, detailing the configuration process for automating HR tasks. It aims to streamline new-hire processes within organizations.

item.title: I tested Gemini on Android Auto and now I can't stop talking to it: 5 tasks it nails
item.description: The author shares their positive experience using Gemini on Android Auto, highlighting five key tasks it performs well. This integration showcases the potential of AI in enhancing in-car experiences.

item.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
item.description: This post discusses the customization of serverless models in Amazon SageMaker AI, focusing on fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 for tool calling. It covers various technical aspects, including dataset preparation and evaluation.

item.title: Building Intelligent Search with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon OpenSearch for hybrid RAG solutions
item.description: The article explains how to build an intelligent search system using Amazon Bedrock and OpenSearch, integrating generative AI for hybrid solutions. This approach combines semantic and text-based search capabilities.

item.title: From isolated alerts to contextual intelligence: Agentic maritime anomaly analysis with generative AI
item.description: Windward's blog post illustrates how generative AI can enhance maritime anomaly analysis by merging geospatial intelligence with AI, allowing analysts to focus on decision-making instead of data collection.

item.title: I used a single power station to keep my off-grid cabin running - how it all worked out
item.description: The author reviews the Bluetti Apex 300 power station, detailing its effectiveness in powering an off-grid cabin. This product is highlighted as a top choice for off-grid living enthusiasts.

item.title: Connecting MCP servers to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway using Authorization Code flow
item.description: This article provides a guide on connecting MCP servers to the Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway using the Authorization Code flow, detailing the setup process for managing AI agent connections.

item.title: Microsoft Agent Framework Explained: Architecture, Workflows, and .NET Use Cases
item.description: The article explains the Microsoft Agent Framework for .NET developers, covering its architecture, workflows, and practical use cases. It includes visual aids to help understand its components.

item.title: Robinhood Ventures has rebounded 30% since its lackluster debut. Can the new private markets fund now withstand the mega IPOs?
item.description: Robinhood Ventures has seen a 30% rebound since its initial public offering, raising questions about its ability to handle upcoming major IPOs from companies like SpaceX and OpenAI.

item.title: Dew Drop – April 6, 2026 (#4640)
item.description: This article provides a roundup of top links and updates in tech, including the latest release of Visual Studio Code and new MAI models.

item.title: Hope for a U.S.-Iran deal, Apple's anniversary, OpenAI's podcast deal and more in Morning Squawk
item.description: The article outlines five key updates for investors, including potential U.S.-Iran negotiations and OpenAI's podcast deal.

item.title: cozempic 1.6.7
item.description: This article discusses enhancements in Claude Code, focusing on improved context management and monitoring tools for effective weight-loss applications.

item.title: Anthropic to bill Claude users separately for OpenClaw usage
item.description: Anthropic has announced that Claude users will be charged separately for OpenClaw usage, shifting to a usage-based pricing model.

item.title: Iran is threatening to bomb the $30 billion Stargate AI data center backed by OpenAI, Nvidia, and other tech giants
item.description: Iran has threatened missile strikes on the Stargate AI data center, which is backed by OpenAI and Nvidia, raising security concerns for major tech investments.

item.title: Agentic AI in practice: Lessons from real deployments
item.description: Martin Bufi shares insights from real-world AI deployments, emphasizing the importance of multi-agent architecture and code-driven strategies for scalability.

item.title: OpenAI calls for robot taxes, a public wealth fund, and a 4-day workweek to tackle AI disruption
item.description: OpenAI has proposed significant policy changes, including robot taxes and a four-day workweek, to address the economic impacts of AI advancements.

item.title: mcp-llama-swap 1.0.0
item.description: The article introduces the MCP server for hot-swapping llama.cpp models within Claude Code sessions, enhancing flexibility in AI applications.

item.title: mcp-llama-swap added to PyPI
item.description: This piece announces the addition of the MCP-llama-swap to PyPI, facilitating the hot-swapping of llama.cpp models in Claude Code sessions.

item.title: Brand new M5 MacBook Air drops to Amazon all-time lows at nearly $150 off, deals from $950
item.description: Amazon is offering significant discounts on the new M5 MacBook Air, with prices dropping nearly $150, marking all-time lows. This aggressive pricing follows a recent spike in Apple product prices.

item.title: Germany's AI Powerhouses: Top 10 Companies Leading Innovation in 2026
item.description: Germany's AI sector is thriving, with nearly 1,000 startups raising billions and making strides in various technologies. This growth positions Germany as a leading hub for AI innovation in Europe by 2026.

item.title: Show HN: Lilith-zero: Ultra-fast, Rust-based security middleware for MCP
item.description: Lilith Zero is a new Rust-based security middleware aimed at enhancing security in LLM-based agent systems. It operates at the transport layer to enforce security policies and prevent data breaches.

item.title: superharness 1.10.2
item.description: The release of superharness 1.10.2 introduces a multi-agent session handoff framework for Claude Code and Codex CLI, enhancing functionality in collaborative coding environments.

item.title: CriticalRiver in pact with Anthropic for enterprise Claude rollout
item.description: CriticalRiver has partnered with Anthropic to facilitate the enterprise rollout of Claude, gaining access to training materials and priority support. This collaboration aims to enhance enterprise AI solutions.

item.title: The real impact of AI on SaaS isn’t what investors think
item.description: The impact of AI on SaaS is expected to lead to lower costs and simpler coding, potentially driving a surge in new software development rather than diminishing it.

item.title: Trader Joe's Onion Flowers
item.description: Trader Joe's has introduced a Bloomin' Onion-inspired appetizer, expanding its product offerings. This move taps into popular food trends and customer preferences.

item.title: mahanai 3.0.0
item.description: MahanAI 3.0.0 is a terminal AI agent designed for NVIDIA NIM, compatible with OpenAI, and includes various tools and safety prompts for enhanced user experience.

item.title: How the iPhone 18 Pro Max Redesigns the Dynamic Island for a 2026 Launch
item.description: Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro Max is set to feature a redesigned Dynamic Island and various performance upgrades, aiming to reshape the smartphone market in 2026.

item.title: Profound vs. AthenaHQ AI: Which AEO platform fits your growth stack?
item.description: With a 600% increase in AI-referred traffic since January 2025, marketers are evaluating tools like Profound and AthenaHQ to enhance brand discovery and pipeline management.
